codeReview.summary: The change matches the feature scope: it narrows RecordId normalization from recursive traversal to root-only handling at the reload/public-output seam, preserving RecordId-backed model roundtrips while avoiding nested payload coercion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure still tells workers to write fail-first tests even for narrowing/evidence-style fixes where precise acceptance regressions already exist, and this worker explicitly deviated by relying on the pre-existing focused tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton + handoff.skillFeedback.deviations for session e209339d-f131-4bc1-8c97-2451574ab191 state they reused existing focused regressions instead of adding failing tests first; .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md step 6-7 currently frames tests-first/fail-first as the default procedure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission instructions require GitNexus impact analysis before edits, but the available environment for this worker did not expose callable GitNexus tooling, forcing a git diff/file-inspection fallback that is only documented indirectly in mission guidance and handoff notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations notes no callable GitNexus interface was available; mission and repo AGENTS/CLAUDE instructions require GitNexus impact analysis before edits.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit f68334e. The implementation cleanly narrows root RecordId normalization in core/src/repository/mod.rs, core/src/serde_utils/id.rs, and core/src/lib.rs, fitting the requested scope and reducing prior regression risk without exposing blocking issues.
